description: When running /cleanup on a file, NEVER shortcut by delegating the whole file to a single lean4:proof-golfer or other batched agent. The /cleanup skill's Phase 4 explicitly mandates ONE worker per declaration with the full 18-item per-declaration audit checklist (HAVE SCAN, SIMP SQUEEZE, NAMING, LINE PACKING, BY PLACEMENT, FORMAT, COMMENTS, DOCSTRING [private/aux → none], VISIBILITY, STRUCTURE, MATHLIB search, JUNK DEF, EXISTS LEMMA, SHOW vs CHANGE, PUSH_NEG, GENERALISE). A single batched golfer drops audit items — most commonly: (a) the 'private lemmas should have NO docstrings' rule (item 10), (b) the STRUCTURE rule that flags >30-line proofs for decomposition (item 12), and (c) the per-decl LINT-warning targeting (item 1). Symptom: every private lemma in the cleaned file has a /-- ... -/ docstring; long proofs were not flagged; the agent reports 'top 5 wins' rather than 'N separate audit reports'. Fix: follow /cleanup's actual Phase 4 pattern — one Agent call per declaration with the verbatim worker prompt — not a shortcut.
